enrichment.summary: Kyle describes his diet while getting back into exercise shape, emphasizing eating regularly and in large enough quantities to fuel workouts and recovery. He outlines his typical meals—morning oatmeal with raisins and honey, protein drinks, bananas, vegetables at night, and energy goos on bike rides—and shares his Costco bulk-buying strategy for cheap staples like frozen vegetables, rice, nuts, and yogurt. He also recounts his actual food intake that day and discusses his ongoing recovery from intense exercise days.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Eating regularly and enough is the most important thing — skipping meals while exercising is a serious mistake
Oatmeal is ideal in the morning — cheap, healthy, detoxifying; vegetables should be avoided in the morning as they don't provide energy
Best eaten at night; a bowl of vegetables in the evening is always a good choice
Better to overeat at night and risk insomnia than to under-eat and hurt recovery
Not a concern — extra fat is stored energy and fuel for endurance, it will burn off on long rides
Tries to be vegetarian; believes it's healthier and kinder to animals, though acknowledges it's challenging for athletes
Best way to shop — spend ~$300-350 once a month and have cheap food for two months
Regrets having one; they interfere with sleep and should be avoided
